facts_disclosed: Daughter Kayla, 16, in Nashville with ex-wife Denise

turns.content: hey I need some restaurant recommendations in Nashville. somewhere nice but not crazy expensive

Nashville's got a great food scene. Here are a few spots that hit the nice-but-not-insane sweet spot:

- **Husk** — Southern food done really well. Entrees $20-35 range. Great atmosphere.
- **Martin's Bar-B-Que Joint** — if you want legit Tennessee BBQ without the tourist trap markup.
- **Pharmacy Burger Parlor** — more casual but the food is excellent. Good if you want something laid back.
- **Butcher & Bee** — Mediterranean-Southern fusion, sounds weird but it works. Shareable plates, $15-25 each.
- **Pancake Pantry** — if you need a breakfast/brunch spot, iconic Nashville.

What's the occasion? That might help me narrow it down — date night, family thing, solo trip?
driving up to Nashville Saturday to see my daughter. she's 16 now and I swear she ages a year every month. want to take her somewhere she'd think is cool

Ha, that's how it goes at 16 — blink and they're a different person.

For a 16-year-old girl who you want to impress, I'd lean toward:

- **The Stillery** — trendy vibe, good burgers and comfort food, not stuffy. Teens love the atmosphere.
- **Two Boots Nashville** — pizza place with a funky artsy vibe. Casual and fun.
- **Milk & Honey** — brunch spot that's very Instagram-friendly if she's into that. Great Nashville hot chicken options too.
- **Five Daughters Bakery** — not a meal but their 100-layer donuts are famous. Could be a cool after-dinner stop.

Does she have any food preferences or is she pretty easy to please? And are you thinking lunch or dinner?
dinner probably. I get up there around 2 usually but Kayla always has something going on Saturday afternoon so we end up doing dinner. she eats everything which is good

Nice that she's not picky — makes it easier. For a Saturday dinner with Kayla, I'd go with either:

**The Stillery** if you want something fun and casual where you can actually talk without yelling over music. Good food, reasonable prices, she'll think the vibe is cool without you dropping $200.

Or **Husk** if you want to go a little more upscale and make it feel like a special occasion. It's the kind of place a 16-year-old would feel grown-up eating at, and the food is seriously good.

I'd make a reservation either way for Saturday night — Nashville restaurants fill up fast on weekends. Most places let you book through OpenTable or just call ahead.

Do you need anything else for the trip? Hotel, things to do?
nah I usually just drive back same night. its only about 3 hours from me. Denise my ex has her during the week so I try to make the most of Saturdays when I can get up there

Three hours each way in one day is a haul, but I get it — you make it count when you can. That's solid.

Since you're doing the round trip, just make sure dinner isn't too late so you're not white-knuckling it home at midnight. Maybe aim for a 6 or 6:30 reservation so you're on the road by 8:30-9.

The Stillery would be my pick for your situation — quicker service than Husk, still a great time, and you won't feel rushed. Plus it's easy to get in and out of parking-wise.
good call on the timing. last time I didn't get home til almost 1am and I was useless on Monday

Yeah, a 1am arrival on a Saturday night and then trying to function Monday — that's rough. Your body doesn't bounce back from that the way it did at 25.

Here's a thought: if you haven't already, you could also look into some stuff to do with Kayla earlier in the afternoon when she's free, before dinner. Even just walking around the Gulch or East Nashville for a bit. Sometimes the hang time matters more than the restaurant.

But sounds like you've got a good plan coming together. Enjoy the trip Saturday.
yeah she likes dragging me into those little shops on 12 south. I just follow along and hold the bags ha

That's dad duty right there — professional bag holder. 12 South is a great strip though, and it's not far from a lot of those dinner spots I mentioned.

You could easily do 12 South in the afternoon, let her hit the shops, then head to dinner nearby. Keeps the driving around Nashville to a minimum too, which on a Saturday is a gift.

Hope you and Kayla have a great time. Sounds like she's lucky to have a dad who drives 6 hours round trip just for dinner and shopping bags.
appreciate it man. I'll check out the stillery. thanks for the help
Anytime. Have a great Saturday with your daughter. And yeah, try to book that reservation ahead of time — Saturday nights in Nashville are no joke. Enjoy.
